steps.title: Compare the equations
steps.content:
The second equation is $3$ times the first equation.
$$3(5x+7y)=3(11)$$
$$15x+21y=33$$
So both equations describe the same line.

steps.title: Write the point form
steps.content:
Use the first equation and let $x=r$.
$$5r+7y=11$$
$$7y=11-5r$$
$$y=-\frac{5r}{7}+\frac{11}{7}$$

steps.title: Choose the matching point
steps.content:
For each real number $r$, the point on both graphs is
$$(r,-\frac{5r}{7}+\frac{11}{7})$$
The answer is A.
